title: Callipteris chocoensis
taxonomy: Callipteris chocoensis is classified within the family Matoniaceae. This fern is classified as a species within the genus Callipteris. The kingdom it belongs to is Plantae. The division it falls under is Pteridophyta. It is also classified as a type of vascular plant.
uses: Callipteris chocoensis has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues and fever. The plant's extracts are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its healing effects. In some parts of South America, Callipteris chocoensis is used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers. Additionally, the plant is said to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Callipteris chocoensis as a medicinal agent.
